                                                                                                                                                                            ESTABLISHMENT DATA
                                                                                                        Seasonal Factors

                 The following tables preset>t seasonal adjustment factors for all series in the establishment section of this periodical. These factors were derived using data through August 1976. The factors should be
           used with current estimates (adjusted to the March 1974 benchmarks) beginning September 1976 for all series shown in the following table except those that relate to labor turnover.
                 The seasonal movements are measured in order to adjust the data statistically for such recurring events as warm and cold weather, crop-growing cycles, holidays, vacations, regular industry model
           changeover periods, and the like. These movements are generally the largest single component of month-to-month change in employment, hours, earnings, and labor turnover. The seasonal factors which
           follow enable the analyst to remove these influences from the data in order to determine more basic trends.

           CONCEPTS                                                                   The civilian labor force comprises the total of all civilians

                                                                                        of the calendar year. The collection agency returns the schedule
                Payroll reports provide current information on wage and                 to the respondent each month so that the next month's data
            salary employment, hours, earnings, and labor turnover in                   can be entered. This procedure assures maximum comparability
            nonagricultural establishments, by industry and geographic                  and accuracy of reporting, since the respondent can see the
            location.                                                                   figures he has reported for previous months.
                                                                                             Form BLS 790 provides for entry of data on the number of
            Federal-State cooperation                                                   full- and part-time workers on the payrolls of nonagricultural
                                                                                        establishments and, for most industries, payroll and hours of
               Under cooperative arrangements with State agencies, the                  production and related workers or nonsupervisory workers for
           respondent fills out a single employment or labor turnover                   the pay period which includes the 12th of the month. Form DL
           reporting form, which is then used for national, State, and                  1219 provides for the collection of information on the total
           area estimates. This eliminates duplicate reporting on the                   number of accessions and separations, by type, during the
           part of respondents, and together with the use of identical                  calendar month.
           techniques at the national and State levels, insures maximum
           comparability of estimates.
               State agencies mail the forms to the establishments and                 CONCEPTS
           examine the returns for consistency, accuracy, and complete-
           ness. The States use the information to prepare State and area              Industrial classification
           series and then send the establishment data to the BLS for use in
           preparing the national series.                                                  Establishments reporting on Form BLS 790 and Form DL
